heading: Submission of legislative proposals
label: 1912 Submission of legislative proposals
text: If the Secretary of Agriculture, after such consultation and receipt of such advice as provided in , determines that additional legislative authority is necessary to develop agricultural programs involving supply adjustments or marketing regulations through the use of marketing orders, marketing quotas or price-support programs, he shall formulate specific recommendations in the form of proposed legislation which shall be submitted to the Congress together with a statement setting forth the purpose and need for such proposed legislation.section 1911 of this title
